comments.body: Does the :annotationprocessor project have the appropriate service-configuration resource? It would named META-INF/services/javax.annotation.processing.Processor and contain the fully-qualified name of your processor implementation class.

To provide more info, you didn't need to do that in the original attempt (without Gradle) because you were specifying the processor class via the -processor argument. Gradle doesn't pass that argument automatically. So you needed to provide the service-configuration resource because the compiler otherwise relies on the ServiceLoader mechanism. That said, you could probably instead configure the compileJava task to pass a -processor argument.

answers.body: As Slaw said, I needed to create a file named javax.annotation.processing.Processor in the directory annotationprocessors\src\main\resources\META-INF\services and put the fully qualified class-name of the processor into that file.

title: Self-contained java annotation-processing with gradle
body:
I am reading into java compile-time annotation-processing.

I can easily process annotations manually on the commandline, using

javac -d . MyAnnotation.java
javac -d . MyAnnotationProcessor.java
javac -d . -processor org.example.MyAnnotationProcessor MyProgram.java

now I'm trying to port this to gradle, but the examples I find online usually only show how to apply existing annotation-processors form mavencentral or using the gradle plugin net.ltgt.apt, which has been discontinued since support for annotation processing has been added to gradle itself.

Lacking good examples, I'm trying to figure out how to do this myself, but I don't know groovy and gradle that well, so I'm mostly guessing. Best I could do was having 3 projects (annotations, annotationprocessors and app), with

dependencies {
    compileOnly project(':annotations')
    annotationProcessor project(":annotationprocessors")
}

in the main app's build.gradle.

With this, the project builds, but the processor doesn't seem to be run. No console output is shown and no files are generated.
